channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: Saros: The Best Weapon to Melt Bosses (Ripsaw Chakram Guide)
item.content:encoded:
While some of the shotguns are very solid weapon choices in the early game thanks to their powerful Alt-Fire modes, there comes a point in Saros where staggering an enemy is no longer enough. To clear the endgame biomes, defeat the final Overlords, and clear the game with ease, you will need the absolute best weapon: the Ripsaw Chakram.

The Ripsaw Chakram is a high-skill, high-reward weapon that functions differently from anything else in Arjun’s arsenal. Once you "unwire" your brain from using the other more traditional weapons in the game, you will realize this is arguably the most powerful weapon in the game.

Table of contents

  • The Ripsaw vs. The Myriad Charkrams
  • The Tormented Retract Alt-Fire, and How It Melts Bosses
  • How to Maximize Damage Output
  • The God Tier Trait: Chakram Blast
  • Why Switch from Other Weapons?

The Ripsaw vs. The Myriad Charkrams

There are two main types of Chakrams in the game, and while they both have their uses, one of them is the superior variant.

  • Myriad Chakram: Uses remotely controlled discs that bounce between enemies. Great for crowds, but lacks the single-target melt factor you need to clear the most difficult challenges of the game.
  • Ripsaw Chakram: Launches discs that embed themselves directly into the enemy. Once embedded, they deal continuous damage over time.

The Tormented Retract Alt-Fire, and How It Melts Bosses

The reason the Ripsaw Chakram is the absolute best weapon for bosses lies in its Alt-Fire mode: Tormented Retract. This allows you to deal massive damage while focusing 100% of your attention on movement and defense.

  • By holding the R2 button halfway (without fully pressing it), you rev the embedded discs.
  • Revving significantly increases both the Damage and the Stagger the discs deal while they are stuck in the enemy.
  • You can embed your discs into any enemy, Overlords included, move to safer locations to avoid their bullet-hell patterns, and simply hold R2 to watch their health bar deplete.

How to Maximize Damage Output

To reach Weapon of Mass Destruction status with the Ripsaw Chakram, there's a certain rhythm you should master.

  1. Launch your discs into the enemy
  2. Hold R2 halfway to tick down their health and build Stagger.
  3. The discs don't stay embedded for long, and will eventually return to you. Before this happens, however, you can press the R2 button fully to unleash the Power Weapon, recalling the discs so that they can be launched at the enemy again.

The God Tier Trait: Chakram Blast

The only limitation of a base Ripsaw is the need to launch discs one by one. However, if you find a version with the Chakram Blast trait, the weapon becomes broken. This trait launches all available discs simultaneously, increasing the damage over time they deal as well as the revving damage.

Why Switch from Other Weapons?

While you may be more comfortable with other weapons, there are plenty of reasons to switch over. The Ripsaw Chakram allows you to clear the most challenging Infernal Strands and endgame biomes while taking very few risks. It takes some practice to manage the R2 trigger pressure (unless you change how to use Alt-Fire), but once it clicks, you will be able to rip through Overlords with ease.

This concludes the guide. For more help, visit our Saros: Complete Walkthrough & Guide hub.

Guide based on a completed playthrough on base PlayStation 5. Screenshots captured from the same version.

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/saros-the-best-weapon-to-melt-bosses-ripsaw-chakram-guide/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Rebellion’s Atomfall Being Turned Into TV Series
item.content:encoded:
A TV adaptation of Atomfall is officially in the works, with Two Brothers Pictures attached to produce the series.

Developed by Rebellion, Atomfall is a post-apocalyptic adventure set in the British Lake District. The game follows a protagonist suffering from amnesia as they navigate a mysterious Quarantine Zone in the aftermath of a nuclear disaster.

More On The Atomfall Series

The news was broken exclusively by Deadline, as they revealed that the show will be co-produced by Two Brothers and Rebellion. Harry and Jack Williams, Emmy and Golden Globe-winning writer-producers, will write the series.

Speaking on the adaptation, the pair highlighted the game’s distinctly British identity and atmospheric setting, noting how exciting it has been to expand its unsettling world for television—particularly as two brothers collaborating with another pair of brothers.

Rebellion founders Jason Kingsley and Chris Kingsley will serve as executive producers. The duo praised the writers’ passion for the source material, revealing that they were impressed by their personal experiences with the game and their own interpretations of its ending.

The project is in its earliest stages of development, as there’s no suggestion of casting details or series length, let alone a potential release window for the show. Even so, Atomfall joins an ever-growing list of video game adaptations, with the likes of God of War and Tomb Raider also in production.

Will you be watching the Atomfall TV series when it eventually arrives? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, check out PlayStation’s response to the DRM issues, as well as the Invincible VS day one patch notes.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/rebellions-atomfall-being-turned-into-tv-series/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox "has work to do", but is "recommitting" to core fans following hardware revenue drop of 33% year-on-year
item.content:encoded:
Player growth has "not yet met our ambition".

Earlier today, Microsoft shared its earnings results Q3 FY2026, covering for the period between 1st January and 31st March. Microsoft's revenue is up 18 percent, at $82.9bn, though gaming revenue fell seven percent. Xbox content and services also saw a drop of five percent year on year. Microsoft attributed this to "a prior year comparable that benefited from strong first-party performance".

Meanwhile, Xbox hardware revenue dropped 33 percent. This follows a price rise for Xbox Series X/S consoles in the US towards the end of last year, the consoles' second in six months. In November, Microsoft said this price increase was due to "changes in the macroeconomic environment". Despite this, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella said the company had "set new records for monthly Xbox active users in the quarter, as well as game streaming hours".

Writing on social media platform X, Microsoft's newly-appointed Xbox boss Asha Sharma said "while we have made progress expanding the business and our margins, player and revenue growth has not yet met our ambition. We know we have work to do to earn every player today and into the future". Nadella also affirmed Xbox will be "recommitting to [its] core fans".

Said Nadella: "When it comes to our consumer business, we're doing the foundational work required to win back fans and strengthen engagement across Windows, Xbox, Bing, and Edge. In the near term we are focused on fundamentals, prioritising quality and serving our core users better. You see this in the work underway across our consumer products. With Windows, we recently announced performance improvements for lower memory devices, streamlined the Windows Update experience, and brought back focus to core features and fundamentals that matter most to our customers. And you also see this in Xbox, where the team is recommitting to our core fans and players and shaping the future of play.

"Last week's Game Pass changes are one example of how we are staying responsive to customer feedback. Monthly Active Windows devices surpassed 1.6 billion... Bing monthly active users reached one billion for the first time."

Earlier this month, Microsoft dropped the price of Xbox Game Pass Ultimate. In the US, the price went from $29.99 per month to $22.99 per month, while PC Game Pass was reduced from $16.49 per month to $13.99 per month. For UK subscribers, Game Pass Ultimate changed from £22.99 per month to £16.99 per month. PC Game Pass, which was £13.49 per month, dropped down to £10.99 per month.

These Game Pass changes are one of the most significant improvements Sharma has implemented since taking on her new role at Xbox. The newly-appointed exec has also scrapped the "This is an Xbox" campaign, and started pointing attention to the next generation of Xbox hardware, so far known only as Project Helix. This hardware will "lead in performance", Sharma said, though when exactly we will see it remains something of a mystery.

As Sharma continues work on fixing the Xbox brand image, there is a growing call from the BDS movement to boycott the gaming platform due to parent company Microsoft's reported complicity in Israeli war crimes against the Palestinian population and state.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/microsoft-xbox-recommitting-hardware-revenue-drop-fy2026-q3
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nine years later, Injustice 3 is reportedly finally in development
item.content:encoded:
A new report has claimed that Mortal Kombat developer NetherRealm Studios is working on Injustice 3, a new installment to the popular DC fighting game franchise.

In a new article on Mp1st, a blurred screenshot of an Warner Bros artist's online resume lists Injustice 3 as a one of the games the developer has worked on, alongside Hogwarts Legacy 2 and two unannounced projects. Eurogamer can not verify the validity of this screenshot, nor the resume itself.

The vast majority of the resume screenshot has been blurred out by Mp1st "for confidentiality reasons". This is not the first indication that Injustice 3 is in the works at NetherRealm. Last year, voice actors for Superman and Aquaman reportedly told members of the public they had worked on the game.

If this report is accurate, it would come roughly nine years after the release of Injustice 2. This beloved, if imperfect, fighting game garnered a lot of love from genre fanatics and DC fans alike, and it was the first fighting game to top UK sales charts in years following its release.

So how likely is this to be true? We know that NetherRealm Studios is working on something new. Major update support for Mortal Kombat 1 ended in May last year, despite prior statements indicating the game would be built upon for years to come. As part of this announcement, it was explicitly stated that focus would be spent on the studio's "next project", with no specific IP stated outright.

It would also come at an interesting time for NetherRealm. Warner Bros. (with NetherRealm Studios included) was recently acquired by Netflix in an $82.7bn deal. Prior to this, Warner Bros underwent some major restructuring as part of a pivot to refocus on ‘key IP’ like Mortal Kombat and its DC titles.

The two IPs NetherRealm has historically worked on - DC and Mortal Kombat - are both quite popular at the moment. The Mortal Kombat 2 movie coming out next month is getting glowing previews, which may build momentum that Netflix/Warner Bros may want to take advantage of through a new game.

That being said, DC is no slouch either: The Batman and The Penguin are critical and commercial successes when it comes to TV and film, and the comics arm of the company is having something of a renaissance thanks to the darker, grislier ‘Absolute’ run for Batman, Wonder Woman, and more.

All that in mind, it's entirely feasible that Injustice 3 could be in the works, but it's far from official as of writing. Still, if nothing else, it's something for the Gamescom Opening Night Live or Summer Games Fest bingo sheet.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/injustice-3-reportedly-in-development
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: A New Nintendo Native PC Port Is Now Out, Super Smash Bros, And It Has Been 100% Generated By AI
item.content:encoded:
As Nintendo is unlikely to ever release its games on PC, emulation has been the only way for users to enjoy them on hardware other than the original consoles for a long time. However, over the past few years, we have seen the rise of native PC ports, including ports of Zelda: Ocarina of Time and Majora's Mask, which offer advanced features over emulated versions, such as support for higher resolutions.

The latest of these is a native port of the original Super Smash Bros., which also serves as an example of the magnitude of what AI can achieve for a project like this, as it has been 100% generated by AI.

BattleShip is the name of the PC port of the first entry in the series created by Masahiro Sakurai. "The port is a pure C/C++ source tree; every byte of Nintendo-owned data is extracted at build time from a ROM that you supply. If you do not own a legal copy of Super Smash Bros. for the Nintendo 64, you cannot build or run this project," reads the official description on GitHub. As such, it requires the NTSC-U v1.0 ROM to run.

What makes the project interesting, besides being able to experience the first entry in the Super Smash Bros. series natively on PC, is the fact that, as already mentioned, it has been 100% generated using AI. "This is a 100% AI-generated modern port. It took a little over 25 days, with me, Opus 4.6, Opus 4.7, and GPT 5.5 as the only contributors. As of 4/28, there are no >2 day gaps in development. At many points, agents were dispatched and worked to build and test autonomously while I did other things," said developer JRickey.

The project was started for two reasons. The first was for the developer to learn more about how things are actually made in C. The second was to provide a "proof of concept that AI can be used for a task of this magnitude. That is not to say that I gave AI an N64 cartridge and got a PC port out of it — years of work from many people went into the decomp, the 3D engine, asset extraction, and everything else this port stands on. The point I am trying to make is that there are tons of cases like this that are low-hanging fruit and can be done with a little bit of your time using AI".

"I want it to serve as proof that the barrier to making really cool things is incredibly low. Humans have always progressed our knowledge and capabilities by expanding on the work of others. Agentic coding is the newest frontier of that principle. I hope this project serves as inspiration for other people to learn by doing, make something for themselves, and give it freely to others," the developer concluded.

This native PC port of Super Smash Bros. having been created with the help of AI tools is, ironically, something that even Masahiro Sakurai himself could get behind. Last year, the legendary game creator commented on the current state of large-scale development, saying that using generative AI to improve efficiency can make it sustainable in a landscape where traditional game development no longer is.

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/nintendo-native-pc-port-super-smash-bros-ai-generated/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Following in Fallout's footsteps, British post-apocalyptic survival jaunt Atomfall's now getting a TV show too
item.content:encoded:
Well, this is a nice suprise. Just a couple of weeks on from me revisiting it to check out the DLC it'd gotten since my review run, Rebellion have announced that their Cumbrian post-apocalyptic survior Atomfall's being adapted into a TV show.

The show's being developed by Two Brothers Pictures, the UK production company behind the likes of BBC comedy Fleabag and Amazon Prime crime thriller The Assassin. Harry and Jack Williams are writing it for Two Brothers, with Rebellion bosses Jason and Chris Kingsley executive producing.

"Harry and Jack demonstrated a clear love for Atomfall and talked about their own particular endings when they were playing the game," the Kingsley brothers said. "It is always exciting to work with people who share the same passion for creating and telling great stories and we are sure that this partnership will help to deliver a television series that will delight fans of the game and beyond."

There's no release date and while the press release recaps the game's plot, it doesn't appear to confirm whether the show'll be a straight-up retelling of that story. I reckon it'll probably be better off telling its own story of life in the Windscale quarantine zone or the events of the disaster that sets the game's events in motion rather than spending hours following a mute survivalist as they creep around the Lake District and occasionally slap people with a cricket bat.

That said, I definitely think Atomfall's fertile TV show ground. As I mentioned in my revisit feature, I thought its two expansions - Wicked Isle and The Red Strain - did a good job of putting more intriguing narrative meat onto an initially very lean bone that generally preferred subtlety to getting weird. It'll be interesting to see, given British post-apocalyptic media's got a history of leaning darker than its American counterpart, whether this show dips even further into that than the base game did or if it'll go for a gory but generally pretty upbeat vibe akin to Amazon's Fallout show.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/following-in-fallouts-irradiated-footsteps-british-post-apocalyptic-survival-jaunt-atomfalls-now-getting-a-tv-show-too
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: CyvOph joins Fnatic after Veqaj Benching
item.content:encoded:
Fnatic Valorant has announced the signing of Clément ‘CyvOph’ Millard from rivals Team Vitality to complete their roster for VCT EMEA Stage 1.

Cyv0ph replaces Veqaj in Fnatic Valorant roster

CyvOph replaces another Frenchman, after Sylvain ‘Veqaj’ Pattyn was benched after missing Fnatic’s Stage 1 clash with GIANTX due to “health issues.”

The organization shared, “After discussions with Veqaj we have decided to move him to the inactive roster while he takes some time away from competition.”

The news left Fnatic without a piece going into their final match of the regular season against BBL, before their vital Upper-Bracket Semi-Final starts their playoff run, which will decide whether the org gets to play their home event at Valorant Masters London.

So, the roster does not have a lot of time to work out how to best use CyvOph and integrate the Frenchman in the roster. Nevertheless, the player seemed unbothered about the magnitude of the task ahead, sharing his excitement at joining Fnatic on social media.

“Hellllllo, I’m back. I’m so happy to join this club with these exceptional players, and too excited to go on stage with them. Thanks so much for this opportunity. See u tomorrow.”

How will CyvOph fit in at Fnatic?

As a flex player, CyvOph’s flexibility will need to be shown as Fnatic prepares to integrate him into the roster.

Veqaj’s time at Fnatic saw the Frenchman play a selfless role to set up Fnatic’s stars elsewhere, with Kajetan ‘kaajak’ Haremski, Austin ‘crashies’ Roberts, and Emir ‘Alfajer’ Ali Beder taking more responsibility after Timofey ‘Chronicle’ Khromov’s departure.

In that sense, his job may be made easier by the solo nature of certain spots, which will prevent a need for strong synergy on the Defending side.

BBL will be a tough test on Thursday, as the VCT EMEA Kickoff winners shocked Fnatic in their opening contest of the year, winning the split.

The matchup is an interesting test, due to the clash of Fnatic’s methodical tactics and BBL’s outright aggression.

The player will only have a short time to impress, even in the best-case scenario, with Fnatic’s future still uncertain after Veqaj’s benching.

To discuss all things Valorant, join Insider Gaming’s Discord!
For more news, see the backlash for Fnatic’s CS skin gambling website, STASHD.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/fnatic-sign-cyvoph/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Tales of Eternia Remastered Pops Up on Age Rating Website
item.content:encoded:
Tales of Eternia was known as Tales of Destiny II outside of Japan.

Details of an unannounced Tales Of remaster have popped up on the European rating board, PEGI.

As yet, Tales of Eternia Remastered has not been formally announced by publisher Bandai Namco, but according to PEGI (thanks, Gematsu), a remaster of the 2000 PSP game is on the way.

If you're struggling to recall this Tales Of game, you're totally forgiven — Tales of Eternia was actually known as Tales of Destiny II outside of Japan (which, confusingly enough, is a different game from the actual Tales of Destiny 2, which released in 2002). Originally released in 2000, the JRPG starts as a mysterious stranger delivers you an ominous message, and you learn that you have been chosen to step up and defend those who cannot defend themselves. It would later inspire a 13-episode anime series, too.

Curiously, despite previously being exclusive to PlayStation, PEGI only lists Tales of Eternia Remastered as coming to Nintendo Switch. It's unclear if this is a mistake, an incomplete listing, or implicit evidence of a Switch exclusive, but watch this space — hopefully Bandai Namco will confirm soon. It looks like it was slapped with a PEGI 12 for "depictions of non-realistic looking violence towards human and fantasy characters," as well as a little bad language.

A number of Tales Of games have already been remastered, of course, including Tales of Berseria Remastered, Tales of Grace f Remastered, and Tales of Symphonia Remastered. While most fans are delighted to see Eternia join the list, some players are a little more cynical, suggesting Bandai Namco should "slow down these remasters and put some actual effort in," adding, "The Berseria remaster was a joke."

It's unclear right now what modern gaming options have been included, such as the ability to toggle enemy encounters on and off, which are staples of the more recent remastered games.

Last year, Bandai Namco announced a Tales of Remastered Program, starting with Tales of Graces f (although a Tales of Symphonia remaster was released the year prior). At the time, we asked producer Yuki Ishikawa about the very popular Tales of the Abyss, widely-regarded as one of the best Tales games, and whether it might be up for a remaster.

“If [a Tales of the Abyss remaster] could happen and I could share that joy with the rest of the fans, that’s something I’ll work hard towards,” Ishikawa said at the time.

Vikki Blake is a reporter for IGN, as well as a critic, columnist, and consultant with 15+ years experience working with some of the world's biggest gaming sites and publications. She's also a Guardian, Spartan, Silent Hillian, Legend, and perpetually High Chaos. Find her at BlueSky.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/tales-of-eternia-remastered-pops-up-on-age-rating-website
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Injustice 3 Rumored to Be Mortal Kombat Developer NetherRealm’s Next Fighting Game
item.content:encoded:
It’s been nearly three years since Mortal Kombat 1 came out, but developer NetherRealm has yet to announce its next game. However, for some time now fans have speculated that Injustice 3 — the next game in its DC fighting game franchise — is in the works at NetherRealm, and recent reports have backed that up.

MP1st reported that Injustice 3 is in active development at NetherRealm, having spotted an explicit mention of the game on the resume of an undisclosed artist currently working at Warner Bros. Games.

Evidence of the existence of Injustice 3 shouldn’t come as much of a surprise, given voice actors for Injustice characters Superman and Aquaman have teased their involvement in a sequel.

In September 2024, NetherRealm development chief Ed Boon, who is currently on the road promoting the imminent release of Mortal Kombat 2 in theaters, revealed that the studio had decided its next game back in 2021. At the time, Boon said NetherRealm was “still fully committed to supporting Mortal Kombat 1 for a long time to come.” But just eight months later, Warner Bros. Games confirmed no new DLC characters or story chapters would be released for the game — a decision that sparked significant backlash within the community.

Since then, we’ve heard basically nothing from NetherRealm about what’s next. You’d like to think an announcement of some nature won’t be too far off though, if the past is anything to go by. The first game in the series, Injustice: Gods Among Us, launched in 2013, with its sequel, Injustice 2, coming out in 2017. NetherRealm released Mortal Kombat 11 in 2019 and for a time it seemed like the studio would alternate between Mortal Kombat and Injustice games, but it went on to release another Mortal Kombat, the soft reboot that is Mortal Kombat 1, in 2023 instead. We’re due a new Injustice, then, if not this year, then perhaps next year.

Wesley is Director, News at IGN. Find him on Twitter at @wyp100. You can reach Wesley at wesley_yinpoole@ign.com or confidentially at wyp100@proton.me.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/injustice-3-rumored-to-be-mortal-kombat-developer-netherrealms-next-fighting-game
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 'The company as a whole no longer exists:' GreedFall developer Spiders confirms closure
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

April 30, 2026

1 Min Read

GreedFall and The Technomancer developer Spiders has closed down. 

The Parisian studio and parent company Nacon filed for insolvency earlier this year after the latter missed a loan repayment. 

The process allows struggling companies operating under French law to freeze their liabilities for an observation period lasting up to 18 months while that debt is potentially restructured. 

The aim is to provide time for ailing businesses to implement a functional recovery plan, but Spiders has now received confirmation that it is being liquidated. 

"We're going to cut straight to the chase so you're not left wondering: After a long period without clear answers, we have received confirmation that Spiders is being liquidated," reads an update from the studio, posted on Facebook.

"What does it mean? This means the company as a whole no longer exists. We'll cease our functions immediately. The planned DLC will release via Nacon, and then—well, that's it. We're sorry that it's come to this and would like to thank each and every one of you for your support over the years.

Related:Xbox delivers record monthly active users, but hardware revenue is still in decline

"If you have any questions or run into issues with your games, please contact Nacon directly as we'll no longer be able to reply."

According to the Spiders website, the studio employed over 90 people alongside a number of freelancers and authors. 

The company also developed its own in-house game engine dubbed the 'Silk Engine' to facilitate the creation of PC and consoles titles such as Steelrising and GreedFall. 

Game Developer has reached out to Nacon for more information about the closure. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/-the-company-as-a-whole-no-longer-exists-greedfall-developer-spiders-confirms-closure
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 3.7 million players later, UK-made action-survival game Atomfall is getting its own TV show
item.content:encoded:
Today, publisher Rebellion has announced that its 2025 action-survival game, Atomfall, is the latest game to make the transition from console to TV screen. Rebellion and Two Brothers Pictures have announced a television adaptation of Atomfall (which won Best British Game at 2026's BAFTA Game Awards, by the way), but no casting or release window information has been revealed just yet.

There is already some very interesting talent attached to the show behind the scenes, however. The series will be written by Emmy and Golden Globe winning producer/writer duo Harry and Jack Williams (credits on Fleabag, The Assassin, The Missing, Baptiste, The Tourist), and executive producers by the pair, plus Alex Mercer (The Assassin, Maya, The Tourist, Doctor Who). Jason and Chris Kingsley, co-founders of Rebellion, will executive produce the title from the publisher's side of things.

Inspired by the real‑world Windscale disaster of 1957, Atomfall was developed from an original idea by Jason Kingsley, and offers a distinctly British take on the post‑apocalyptic genre. Taking place in a Quarantine Zone established in the Lake District, the game tackles themes of Britain's post-war identity crisis, nuclear paranoia, and the ever-tantalising question of 'what happens if you isolate one small British town from the rest of the outside world'.

"Atomfall has such a distinctive British tone and setting, and it’s been a real joy developing it alongside the Rebellion team - especially as two brothers working alongside two brothers (Jason Kingsley and Chris Kingsley)," said Harry and Jack Williams. "There’s something very exciting about expanding this strange, unsettling story for television."

Jason Kingsley and Chris Kingsley added, via joint statement: "We are delighted to be partnering with Two Brothers to bring the world of Atomfall to television. Harry and Jack demonstrated a clear love for Atomfall and talked about their own particular endings when they were playing the game. It is always exciting to work with people who share the same passion for creating and telling great stories and we are sure that this partnership will help to deliver a television series that will delight fans of the game and beyond."

In his three star review of Atomfall, our own Tom Orry said of the game: "If you can get over a difficult start and fancy a lean take on the survival genre, Atomfall delivers an intriguing tale worth discovering." He carried on to note that it was the gameplay systems that often let the title down, however, and that he "really enjoyed the world that Rebellion created, the duplicitous main characters, and the post-war sci-fi story." Which all bodes well for a TV show, don't you think?

Atomfall

PS4, PS5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X/S, PC

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/atomfall-tv-show-revealed
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PlayStation now requires a ‘one-time online check’ to confirm you own a game
item.content:encoded:
Jess Weatherbed

is a news writer focused on creative industries, computing, and internet culture. Jess started her career at TechRadar, covering news and hardware reviews.

Sony has finally stepped in to clear up the growing confusion around a new DRM (Digital Rights Management) system on PlayStation 5 and PlayStation 4 consoles. Users reported that the system’s latest updates seemingly introduced a requirement to go online once every 30 days to validate game licenses, but Sony now says this is false.

In a statement to GameSpot, a Sony Interactive Entertainment spokesperson said that “a one-time online check is required to confirm the game’s license, after which no further check-ins are required.” The DRM rumors had sparked concerns that gamers who don’t sign in every month would lose access to their games by allowing the licenses to lapse, but Sony says that “players can continue to access and play their purchased games as usual.”

It’s a welcome rebuttal that should give PlayStation gamers some peace of mind, however it comes several days after content creator Modded Hardware first reported the DRM concerns on April 24th. Subsequent screenshots shared online appeared to show a 30-day timer on PlayStation Store purchases made after March 2026.

With no official response from Sony to explain the changes at the time, users speculated that the new DRM update required monthly online connectivity, perhaps to combat refund scams that use jailbroken PS4 consoles to extract digital game licenses. The introduction of a one-time verification could still be aimed at tackling such a vulnerability, though Sony hasn’t confirmed its reasoning behind the changes.

Sony’s slow response has reheated concerns about ownership rights for digital games, however. The messy situation mirrors similar discord from 2013 around Microsoft requiring Xbox One owners to be subjected to online checks every 24 hours. That was swiftly scrapped following feedback from outraged gamers, but not before Sony released an ad poking fun at its competitor’s decisions.

Follow topics and authors from this story to see more like this in your personalized homepage feed and to receive email updates.

  • Jess Weatherbed


https://www.theverge.com/games/921064/sony-playstation-drm-online-license-statement
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dbrand Claims Its New Joy-Con Holder Is More Comfortable Than A Pro Controller
item.content:encoded:
Well, we appreciate the enthusiasm

Accessory manufacturer dbrand has launched a new controller holder for Switch 2 that it claims is more comfortable than a Pro Controller.

It's essentially a plastic case in which to fit your Joy-Con 2 controllers, kinda like the 'JoyVerse' grip from JSAUX, except this one seems to have some sort of hinge to allow for some angle adjustment.

"Introducing a new way to hold your Joy-Cons: the Joy-Lock Controller Holder. It's 80% cheaper than a Pro Controller, yet somehow even more comfortable."

On its own, the controller holder is priced at $19.95, but it's also designed to be used in conjunction with dbrand's 'Joy-Lock Grips', which are also priced at $19.95. Granted, if you get them both together, dbrand will knock $5 off to make it $34.90.

Then you've got some Stick Grips for the analogue controllers for an extra $9.95. So if you go for everything, you're looking at $44.95 which, yes, is about half what you'd expect to pay for an official Pro Controller, but is this thing really more comfortable..?

Honestly, we have our doubts.

And that's nothing against dbrand. Even with some solid grips, the Joy-Con are still, well... Joy-Con. They simply don't compare to the Pro Controller. You gotta be bold when you're trying to sell your wares though, so we admire the enthusiasm.

Here's a look at dbrand's new controller holder:

And for comparison, here's the Pro Controller:

To give dbrand some credit, we actually thought its Killswitch Case for the Switch 2 was pretty great, awarding it a score of 8/10 in our review for its design and comfort.

What do you make of dbrand's new controller holder? Reckon its claim holds any weight? Let us know with a comment.

[source dbrand.com]

Nintendo Life’s resident horror fanatic, when he’s not knee-deep in Resident Evil and Silent Hill lore, Ollie likes to dive into a good horror book while nursing a lovely cup of tea. He also enjoys long walks and listens to everything from TOOL to Chuck Berry.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/04/dbrand-claims-its-new-joy-con-holder-is-more-comfortable-than-a-pro-controller
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PlayStation 5 Linux Breakthrough Lands A Little Late, But It Could Reshape How Some Owners Use Their Consoles
item.content:encoded:
Ever since the system's release in late 2020, developers have been hard at work expanding the PlayStation 5's functionality beyond what Sony intended, helped by leaks that could lead to permanent jailbreaks. Now, over five years since the console's release, those with an older phat system still running older versions of the system software can turn Sony's current generation into a highly capable Linux PC, and take advantage of its Zen 2 8 CPU cores and RDNA 2 GPU to run emulators and Steam games with impressive fluidity thanks to a new loader released online by Andy Nguyen a.k.a. TheFl0w.

"ps5-linux leverages a patched HV vulnerability to transform your PS5 Phat console running 3.xx or 4.xx firmwares into a highly capable Linux PC, unlocking its full hardware potential for desktop use," the loader GitHub description reads. "Powered by 8 CPU cores (16 threads) at 3.5 GHz and a GPU at 2.23 GHz, it provides enough performance to run Steam games and various emulators with impressive fluidity. It supports HDMI 4K60 video and audio output. Furthermore, it allows you to utilize an M.2 SSD as a dedicated Linux partition, as well as all USB ports on the console."

As already mentioned, not many users will be able to run Linux on PlayStation 5 due to limitations: only select firmwares are supported, and only version 4.0 supports M.2.

  • 3.00, 3.10, 3.20, 3.21, without M.2 support
  • 4.00, 4.02, 4.03, 4.50, 4.51 with M.2 support

The developer, however, could be adding support for other firmwares, including 1.xx and 2.xx firmwares. Support for 5.xx firmwares could be coming as well, but since Linux would have to run within GameOS VM, it may result in reduced performance and other limitations.

If you do happen to have an older PlayStation 5 that you did not update, want to try running Linux on the system, but don't want to lose the system's regular functionality, you are in luck, as this modification doesn't touch the internal M.2 drive. As this is a soft-mod, however, you will need to run the exploit every time you want to boot into Linux, with no option to put it in standby.

Since this is a very fresh release, we have yet to see what can be achieved on a PlayStation 5 running Linux, but we already saw a while back that it can run select Steam games not only flawlessly, but with the additional features that are normally not available in native PS5 versions of the game. As shown last month by developer Andy Nguyen, it is possible to run Grand Theft Auto V Enhanced on the system, complete with ray tracing, at 60 FPS, which is the maximum FPS currently supported by the exploit.

Seeing Linux running on a PlayStation 5 is exciting enough for the possibilities it opens, but it's not exactly new for Sony systems. Those who have been playing video games for some time and were around for the PlayStation 3 launch will surely remember OtherOS, an early feature that allowed users to run Linux on the system. This feature was controversially removed by firmware update 3.21 in April, leading to a class action lawsuit against Sony, which was later dismissed in 2011.

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/playstation-5-linux-breakthrough-reshape-consoles/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: American Truck Simulator cruises into Illinois with a fresh expansion releasing mid-May, as SCS also tease a new truck
item.content:encoded:
Your cargo's Chicago-bound

Fresh off adding a new feature that'll make my reckless driving a bit easier to recover from, SCS Software have announced a release date for American Truck Simulator's next expansion, as the game continues its trek from US west coast to East. Illinois is the latest destination and you'll be able to take to its highways on May 14th.

"Illinois showcases the best of what the USA has to offer. Hop into your rig, hit the legendary Route 66, and journey through Springfield, the state’s historic capital, to Chicago, one of the Midwest's most iconic cities," SCS wrote in the DLC's description.

"Travel alongside the mighty Mississippi River, which defines the state’s western border. Discover charming river towns, classic steamboats, and scenic vineyards dotting the landscape. In the southern part of Illinois, experience a blend of cities, lush farmland, and quaint small towns. Meanwhile, the northeastern region is a bustling hub of urban and suburban life, anchored by Chicago, the nation’s third-largest city, nestled on the bank of Lake Michigan."

They add that once Illinois is out, you'll be able to drive the entirety of Route 66 in ATS, with the iconic highway running from the game's initial stomping ground of California all the way through Arizona, New Mexico, Texas, Oklahoma, Kansas, and Missouri before reaching Chicago. I can certainly see myself making a point of driving the length of it once the Road Trip mode SCS announced a while back brings lets me drive a regular car across its version of America.

That said, SCS are clearly still prioritising truckers at the moment, with this DLC date reveal having come alongside a teaser for a new truck set to arrive in the sim at some point soon. The latest version of Volvo's VNL rig looks to be the lorry in question, which is cool, though I do wish ATS had a few more retro-style lorries outside of the likes of the International 9900i and the Kenworth W900 if you opt to deck the latter out in such fashion.

Either way, it'll be interesting to see how big Chicago feels rendered in ATS' scale, as well as breeze through some more midwestern farmland when I fancy a break from runs through the pacific northwestern wilderness.

You can wishlist American Truck Simulator's Illinois expansion on Steam.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/american-truck-simulator-cruises-into-illinois-with-a-fresh-expansion-releasing-mid-may-as-scs-also-tease-a-new-truck
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox delivers record monthly active users, but hardware revenue is still in decline
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

April 30, 2026

2 Min Read

Xbox hardware revenue remains in decline, but Microsoft said it set new records for monthly active users (MAUs) on Xbox during Q3 FY26.

As noted in the company's latest fiscal report, Gaming revenue decreased by $380 million year-on-year—representing a 7 percent decline over that period. That decrease was driven by tumbling Xbox content and services and Xbox hardware revenue. 

In a supplemental SEC filing, Microsoft noted that Xbox content and services revenue decreased by 5 percent year-on-year, while Xbox hardware revenue fell by 33 percent over the same period driven by "lower volume of consoles sold." 

Xbox hardware revenue has been in steady decline for some time, but Microsoft is now looking to reposition and revitalise its video game division under new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma. 

In a memo sent to global Xbox employees earlier this month, Sharma conceded that Xbox is currently a "challenger" as opposed to a market leader. 

Related:Landfall and Aggro Crab team up for Make-a-Wish game bundle

During a Q3 earnings call, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella said the company is currently doing the "foundational work required to win back fans and strengthen engagement" across key businesses, including Xbox. 

"In the near term, we are focused on fundamentals, prioritizing quality and serving our core users better," he said. "[...] And you also see this in Xbox where the team is recommitting to our core fans and players, and shaping the future of play. Last week’s Game Pass changes are one example of how we are staying responsive to customer feedback."

Nadella was referencing the recent decision to lower the price of some Xbox Game Pass tiers. That move came just months after Microsoft chose to increase the cost of the subscription service. 

Sharma recently told Xbox workers that daily active players will be the division's "north star" moving forward. It appears the company is already making progress on that front.

"We set new records for monthly Xbox active users in the quarter, as well as game streaming hours," said Microsoft CFO Amy Hood during the earnings call. 

Looking ahead to Q4, Hood said Xbox revenue is expected to remain in decline across the board. 

"In Xbox content and services, we expect revenue to decline in the low-teens, reflecting a prior year comparable that benefited from strong first-party content, as well as the recent price changes for Xbox Game Pass as we focus on delivering more value to gamers," she added. "Hardware revenue should decline year-over-year."

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/xbox-delivers-record-monthly-active-users-but-hardware-revenue-is-still-in-decline
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Asobo Teases Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy Release Is ‘Almost Here’ After Nearly a Year of Silence
item.content:encoded:
It's been almost a year since Studio Asobo and Focus Home revealed Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy, the third game in the peculiar action/adventure series.

After a long silence, the French studio has shared a lot of info about this prequel, including its development status, in a fresh devblog. There's good news on that front, as Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy is in the final production phase with the last recording sessions and the final notes of the score wrapping up. The blog post teases that "the release is almost here", suggesting it will be released this year, possibly ahead of the behemoth called Grand Theft Auto VI (which is still scheduled for November 19). For the record, the previous game, A Plague Tale: Requiem, launched on October 18, 2022.

Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy is set 15 years before the events seen in Requiem and takes place elsewhere, too: in the Mediterranean Sea, specifically on Minotaur's Island, a location said to conceal a secret treasure. The game shifts between two timelines via a mechanic called "Echoes": 14th-century medieval Greece and ancient Minoan civilization.

The protagonist, Sophia, is also partly new. She appeared as a companion in Requiem, but she'll be playable this time around, and she'll also spearhead the biggest change in the series: as a pirate smuggler, she has access to a full melee combat system with swords, daggers, and parries, whereas the previous protagonist, Amicia, used her sling as a ranged weapon.

The blog post dives into Sophia's different looks in this new game and also provides more context for her backstory. At a young age, she was placed in a convent by her mother's family, but escaped to her father, the leader of a pirate camp, where she was raised and trained to fight, eventually earning the nickname "The Scorpion of the Seas". The story shifts between her adult life and childhood flashbacks, exploring her motivations and the world she grew up in. Moreover, the Macula (the rat plague curse at the heart of the series) is directly linked to events on Minotaur's Island, and the devblog hints that Sophia herself may be affected by it, setting up deeply personal stakes for the story.

Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy also features complex puzzles that will require using a stolen Minoan sphere to solve by manipulating light, and there's something restless lurking in the shadows of the island that follows Sophia around, increasing tension.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/resonance-plague-tale-legacy-release-almost-here/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For April 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, April 30, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Metal alloy
  • Hint 2: Made from copper and tin
  • Hint 3: Represents third place

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Metal
  • Antique
  • Pewter
  • Plate
  • Nickel
  • Gold
  • Silver
  • Copper
  • Brass

Today’s Contexto Answer For April 30, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Bronze

Bronze is a brown metal alloy made of copper and tin.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on May 1.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

  • Wordle

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/todays-contexto-hints-answer-for-april-30-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sony’s PlayStation DRM Mess Gets an Official Answer After Xbox One 2013 Comparisons Spread Across the Internet
item.content:encoded:
A Sony spokesperson has finally provided an official response to the PlayStation Online DRM controversy. Gamespot received the following statement after asking for some much-needed clarification:

Players can continue to access and play their purchased games as usual. A one-time online check is required to confirm the game's license, after which no further check-ins are required.

The controversy began in late April when several PlayStation users noticed a 30-day countdown timer appearing on the license information page of newly purchased digital games on PS4 and PS5. The timer displayed a "Valid Period" start and end date, along with "Remaining Time," sparking widespread fears that digital game licenses would expire if consoles weren't connected to the internet during that window.

The issue was first flagged publicly by YouTuber Modded Hardware, subsequently verified by DoesItPlay (a game preservation-focused account), and later shared widely by prominent modder Lance McDonald. The feature appears to affect only games purchased after the March 2026 system update, with older purchases remaining unaffected.

Initial responses from PlayStation's chat support made things worse, not better. Some support representatives confirmed the 30-day requirement was intentional and not a bug, while others told users no recurring check-in would be needed; these contradictory answers obviously amplified panic. PlayStation Support also confirmed that setting a console as "Primary" does not bypass the requirement.

The situation drew immediate comparisons to Microsoft's Xbox One DRM debacle in 2013, when the company infamously required an online check-in every 24 hours before backing down under massive public pressure.

Sony's statement to GameSpot appears to clarify that the timer is a one-time authentication mechanism, a temporary license that converts to permanent after the initial online check, rather than a recurring 30-day requirement. It has been suggested that the change may be aimed at combating fraud, potentially linked to refund scams or exploits.

However, Sony has not explained why the change was rolled out silently and without any public announcement, which remains the central criticism even after the clarifying statement. No doubt this could have been avoided with clearer and faster communication.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/sony-playstation-drm-official-response-one-time-check/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Ideals Like Full-Time Employment and Job Security Are ‘Too Romantic’ in Game Dev, Says Monument Valley Studio CEO
item.content:encoded:
The CEO of Monument Valley developer Ustwo Games, Maria Sayans, has outlined the studio needs to lower development costs, and to do so it will rely on contractors in the future rather than hiring full-time staff. Sayans explained the plan in an interview with Game Developer at London Games Fest.

In the wake of Netflix dropping the Monument Valley games from its service and deals with major companies drying up, Ustwo has a plan to focus on building “meaningful single player experiences” for PC and consoles. It’s a plan already in motion, with Ustwo recently porting several of its games to platforms like Steam and Switch without publisher support.

However, Sayans explained that they’ve subsequently learned that lower development budgets are going to be crucial.

“We saw a lot of potential for the Monument Valley IP to be maybe reset and reinvented for PC and consoles, but what became clear was that our development budgets were too high for us to achieve a safer break even if we were aiming for PC and console,” she said, explaining that the studio has been making games over three- to four-year production cycles for between £7 million to £10 million, and that they have to lower that cost – especially if and when working on new projects outside the established Monument Valley series.

“For example, if we did something like Alba or Assemble With Care, we would have to do that for a lot less money,” said Sayans. “There are people doing really, really well in those spaces on PC for much smaller budgets, that we will never be able to achieve because we’re based in London and have employees with pensions and so on.

“We’ve been a little bit too romantic about the idea that we should have employees and give people long-term job security. I think that got us into a place where, reaching the heights of Monument Valley 3 [production], contractors were always a relatively low percentage of our employee base.”

Sayans said Ustwo will be changing this balance in the future.

“I think going forward, we’ll see that we’ve got a core team and any growth will come through contractors, which is something I hate about the industry. I’ve been in the industry for 20 years, and those of us who joined in the early 2000s, we had it very good. You want to be able to give that kind of stability... but I think that's a shift in how we want to work with people going forward.”

Luke is a Senior Editor on the IGN reviews team. You can track him down on Bluesky @mrlukereilly to ask him things about stuff.



https://www.ign.com/articles/ideals-like-full-time-employment-and-job-security-are-too-romantic-in-game-dev-says-monument-valley-studio-ceo
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up? Fortnite April 30 Downtime, Explained
item.content:encoded:
While Fortnite remains in the midst of Chapter 7, Season 2, a fresh update is arriving on April 30, meaning that the servers for the Epic Games title will be unavailable for a period of time.

Epic Games’ regular schedule of a new Fortnite update every two weeks has become a core part of the game, and following on from the last update on April 16, there’s fresh downtime taking place.

If you’re itching for your Fortnite fix and want to know when the servers will be available again, Insider Gaming has all the details.

When Will Fortnite Servers Be Back Up?

Fortnite servers are down for several hours for the April 30 update, although the exact time they will return has not been confirmed by Epic Games.

Downtime on April 30 is expected to begin at 4 AM ET, and while the exact length of time the servers will be unavailable before hasn’t been stated publicly, previous experience provides an expected time frame.

  • More Insider Gaming: Fortnite Offers Refunds for d4vd Items

Mid-season updates don’t have downtime as long as the seasonal transitions, like the move to a new Chapter. Instead, downtime lasts for around three to four hours on average.

As such, the Fortnite servers should be back up around 7 AM to 8 AM ET on April 30, though this is subject to change, particularly if any issues are discovered, and the best way to keep track of the server status is via social media platforms.

What’s in The Fortnite April 30 Update?

The Fortnite update on April 30 is considerably smaller than the previous two mid-season updates, particularly compared to the last update two weeks ago that debuted a new Fortnite Festival season and moved Save the World to a free-to-play model.

Instead, this update is focused largely on small gameplay changes, with fresh weapons in Battle Royale, a new Blitz Map, and a Reload Loot Pool Update.

  • More Insider Gaming: Can You Play Fortnite Save The World on Mobile?

As always, the Shop will be refreshed with new items, including the debut of freshly released cosmetic, and it’s a significant turning point, as it marks the first time that skins from the Absolute Doom Battle Pass from 2024.

However, it’s not clear yet what Fortnite’s plans are for returning skins from that Battle Pass once the exclusivity period ends, and they could be saved for a future drop.

What do you want to see Fortnite add in the next update? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Fortnite, find out if you can still get free V-Bucks in Save the World, and check out how to unlock the free Jess skin.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/fortnite-servers-down-april-30/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Kyoto Xanadu details more characters, Action Cards, Support Cards, and Aether
item.content:encoded:
Falcom has released new information and screenshots for action RPG Kyoto Xanadu introducing characters Utsuho Tatsumiya, Dogi, Suzume Sawatari, Kyoya Suo, Momiji Kisaragi, and Alusalu Minori Milvi, as well as how “Action Cards” and “Support Cards” are utilized in class, and offering Aether to the Sacred Tree.

Get the details below.

■ Characters

Utsuho Tatsumiya (voiced by Jun Fukushima)

  • Age: 35

An instructor at Hirasaka Academy and the homeroom teacher of Rei’s class. He teaches Japanese.

Fundamentally unmotivated, he makes no attempt to hide his languid demeanor, even in front of his students.

He often makes cynical and negative remarks, and at times even says things unbecoming of a teacher.

Dogi (voiced by Kenta Miyake)

  • Age: 34

A large, burly physical education instructor at Hirasaka Academy.

Despite his bold and carefree personality, he is cheerful and attentive, making him a reliable adult admired by students.

Although Dogi himself is not a Chosen, he possesses immense physical strength befitting his appearance—so much so that he can supposedly smash a solid wall to pieces with a single punch.

Suzume Sawatari (voiced by Rumi Okubo)

  • Age: 16
  • Soul Device: ???

A first-year high school student attending Hirasaka Academy as a Chosen.

She is shrewd and calculating, trading strategy data for Hirasaka Xanadu within the academy and independently profiling individuals that may require special caution.

Sensing a business opportunity in Rei, who declared on his first day as a transfer student that he would “conquer the entirety of Hirasaka Xanadu,” she approaches him in an attempt to sell her services.

Kyoya Suo (voiced by Kengo Kawanishi)

  • Age: 24
  • Soul Device: Pistol types

A young man who graduated from Hirasaka Academy and specializes in a tricky fighting style that combines martial arts with a pistol-type Soul Device.

He now works as a freelancer, accepting requests from all sorts of clients to deal with the other world and supernatural phenomena.

Easygoing and somewhat detached from the real world, he collects strange junk as countermeasures against other world encroachment—though this habit has partly turned into a hobby.

Momiji Kisaragi (voiced by You Taichi)

  • Age: 22

A staff member at Hirasaka Academy in charge of reception at the Xanadu Lobby.

Highly capable, she can handle any task with ease and provides thorough support to students taking on Xanadu.

Though not an instructor herself, she is highly trusted by the students, so much so that Rei has remarked she is “more of a teacher than Tatsumiya.”

Alusalu Minori Milvi (voiced by Rico Sasaki)

  • Age: 27

A half-Nordic woman who lives alone in Kyoto. She works as a model and is often seen strolling around the city in stylish outfits.

After Rei moves into the same apartment building, the two begin running into each other frequently as neighbors.

She is friendly and easygoing, and seems perfectly normal, but… from time to time, strange, hair-raising noises can be heard coming from her apartment next door.

■ System – Class: “Action Cards” and “Support Cards”

By taking classes at Hirasaka Academy, you can enhance your spiritual powers of “Wisdom,” “Virtue,” and “Valor,” which are used to strengthen your Soul Devices.

The basic class flow is as follows:

  1. On the “Class Preparation” screen, choose which “Action Cards” and “Support Cards” to use.
  2. Start the class.
  3. Earn Wisdom, Virtue, and Valor points based on the class results.

Action Cards

  • Action Cards can be used during the class preparation phase to enhance your focus, increasing the Wisdom, Virtue, and Valor points earned in class.
  • Focus points and Cost Points (CP) differ depending on the type of Action Card, and Action Cards can be used until your CP is depleted.
  • Cards with high Focus points also have high CP.

Support Cards

  • Support Cards can be used during the class preparation phase to gain beneficial effects.
  • Like Action Cards, some Support Cards require CP, so it is important to decide how to allocate your limited CP between Action Cards and Support Cards.
  • Additionally, once used, a Support Card cannot be reused until it recovers. However, it can be made available again by interacting with the corresponding character.

■ System – Facilities: “Sacred Tree”

By collecting otherworldly “Aether” into your “Aether Vial” during exploration of Xanadu and bringing it back to the real world to offer to the Sacred Tree on the academy grounds, you can receive various blessings.

Aether can be obtained by destroying terrain in Xanadu or by attacking and purifying stone statues corrupted by otherworldly miasma.

★★★ Warning: Collecting Too Much Aether ★★★

  • If you continue collecting Aether after your Aether Vial exceeds its capacity, there is a chance the vial will shatter, causing “Aether Poisoning.”
  • When afflicted with Aether Poisoning, your HP will decrease, Soul Accelerator cannot be used, and HP recovery and item use become unavailable. Symptoms worsen over time, making exploration of Xanadu increasingly difficult.

Kyoto Xanadu is due out for PlayStation 5, Switch 2, Switch, and PC via Steam on July 16 in Japan and Asia. A western release is also planned.

Watch a new set of character trailers below. View the screenshots at the gallery.
